compiled_state.title: As a human reviewer, I can navigate to related tickets from any PR commit
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Inject Jira-Ticket and DSO-Task git trailers into commits produced by the DSO commit workflow, enabling human PR reviewers to navigate to the related JIRA ticket and DSO ticket system entry directly from any commit.

**Why**: Human reviewers currently have no link in commit messages to navigate to the related tickets. SC7 of this epic requires both trailer types to be present on PR commits.

**Scope**:
- IN: Append Jira-Ticket and DSO-Task trailers via git interpret-trailers as the final commit message transformation. Read DSO-Task from active task context (session artifact); read Jira-Ticket from active task SYNC event jira_key field (.tickets-tracker/<task_id>/SYNC* files). Graceful degradation when values are unavailable (no active task, no JIRA sync, missing SYNC file). Update COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md to document the trailer injection step.
- OUT: Full DSO attribution trailer suite (DSO-Skill, DSO-Agent, DSO-Model, etc.) — that is scope of 170b-2ac9 (commit attribution epic). Version bump commit trailer handling — out of scope for this story.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, commits produced by the DSO commit workflow include a Jira-Ticket: <value> git trailer populated from the active task's SYNC event jira_key field; verified via git log -1 --format='%(trailers:key=Jira-Ticket,valueonly)' on a test commit with a JIRA-synced active task
  <- Satisfies: "commits include Jira-Ticket and DSO-Task git trailers; human reviewers can follow either trailer value to the corresponding ticket system"
- When this story is complete, commits include a DSO-Task: <value> git trailer populated from the active session's DSO task ID; verified via git log -1 --format='%(trailers:key=DSO-Task,valueonly)'
  <- Satisfies: SC7 "commits include Jira-Ticket and DSO-Task git trailers"
- When this story is complete, when no active task is present or the task has no JIRA sync, no trailers are appended and the commit workflow exits 0
  <- Satisfies: SC7 "each trailer omitted when source value is unavailable"
- When this story is complete, trailers are appended via git interpret-trailers --trailer as the final commit message transformation, conforming to git trailer block convention (blank line separator, keys not in subject or body)
  <- Satisfies: SC7
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Fail-open is required: if active task context is unavailable, commit proceeds without trailers — never fail the commit due to missing context
- [Testing] SYNC event file may be absent or malformed — handle gracefully (trailer omitted, not error); test this path explicitly
- [Integration] Verified: git interpret-trailers --trailer "Key: value" syntax works correctly on git 2.53.0 (tested in preplanning session)
- [Compatibility] Trailer key names (Jira-Ticket, DSO-Task) must remain consistent with 170b-2ac9 (commit-level source attribution epic) which will later add the full trailer suite
